_note: b_verdict='pass' with only ONE per_signal_results entry (single layer) VIOLATES the B-gate policy (ALG §3.7-5:198: >=2 independent signal layers required to classify a B-layer outcome as pass). But this is an OPEN-class event payload (no additionalProperties:false, and JSON Schema cannot express '>=2 entries with DISTINCT evidence_basis_id' as a PASS precondition), so it CANNOT be schema-rejected — it remains schema-VALID. Enforcing the >=2-independent-layer PASS rule is the w5-06 b_gate guard's policy-gate obligation (wave5-plan.md E4), honestly deferred here and NOT faked in-schema (same gap class as ADR-0024(e)-1 tenant-id-in-payload / wave4 outcome-field-gap).
